title: Leg Pain When Walking - Peripheral Arterial Disease
chief_complaint: 68-year-old male smoker with progressive right calf pain when walking 100 meters, relieved by rest. Cool right foot, hair loss on leg. Diabetes and hypertension.

physical_exam.legs: Cool right foot, hair loss right leg, thickened nails, pallor on elevation, rubor on dependency, right femoral bruit, right DP and PT pulses absent, left palpable

hidden_data.ct_angiogram: Right superficial femoral artery 80% stenosis at Hunter canal, popliteal artery patent, three-vessel runoff

correct_diagnosis: Peripheral Arterial Disease (Right SFA Stenosis) with Claudication
key_learning_points: Claudication: reproducible pain with exercise, relieved by rest
Revascularization if disabling claudication or CLI

patient_responses.pain: My right calf cramps after walking a block, goes away when I stop

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! PAD with classic claudication.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is PAD. The ABI <0.9 + symptoms confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: PAD management: Aspirin 81mg + Atorvastatin 80mg. Supervised exercise therapy (30-45min, 3x/week). Cilostazol for symptom relief. Smoking cessation critical. Revascularization (angioplasty/stent or bypass) if disabling claudication or CLI.
